Description: "Fix renderOrder: black sphere must render FIRST (low renderOrder, depthWrite=true), then lensing sprite renders AFTER (higher renderOrder, depthTest=true) so sphere naturally masks the center"
Instruction: "Fix renderOrder so sphere draws first (renderOrder=1) and lensing sprite draws later (renderOrder=5) with depthTest=true"
TargetContent:
"  return (
    <group>
      {/* ═══ STEP 1: Pure black core sphere ═══
          depthWrite=true so it correctly blocks the depth buffer.
          renderOrder=10 ensures it draws AFTER disks (which have lower renderOrder)
          so the disk can show behind the sphere at the edges. */}
      <mesh renderOrder={10}>
        <sphereGeometry args={[0.63, 64, 64]} />
        <meshBasicMaterial color="#000000" depthTest={true} depthWrite={true} />
      </mesh>

      {/* ═══ STEP 2: Lensing ring + photon ring as billboard Sprite ═══
          depthTest=TRUE: the black sphere (renderOrder=10, depthWrite=true)
          will have written to the depth buffer FIRST (higher renderOrder draws last
          in Three.js default pipeline when transparent=false), so this sprite's
          pixels at the center will fail depth test → naturally blocked → black center!

          Wait — actually renderOrder=10 draws AFTER renderOrder=9.
          So sphere (10) draws after sprite (9). Sprite pixels at center won't be blocked yet.
          
          Solution: render sphere FIRST (lower renderOrder) with depthWrite=true,
          then sprite with depthTest=true at higher renderOrder.
          Sphere renderOrder=2, Sprite renderOrder=5 → sphere writes depth first → sprite masked. */}
      {lensingTex && (
        <sprite ref={lensingRef} scale={[2.0, 2.0, 1]} renderOrder={5}>
          <spriteMaterial
            map={lensingTex}
            transparent
            opacity={1.0}
            blending={THREE.AdditiveBlending}
            depthTest={true}
            depthWrite={false}
          />
        </sprite>
      )}

      {/* ═══ STEP 3: Accretion disks as PlaneGeometry (tilted) ═══
          PlaneGeometry gives correct UV → our horizontal band texture works perfectly */}
      {diskTex1 && (
        <mesh ref={disk1Ref} rotation={[Math.PI / 2.5, 0, 0]} renderOrder={3}>
          <planeGeometry args={[4.2, 1.0, 1, 1]} />
    
<truncated 1424 bytes>
